# Transcrição: 02. Creating a Process of Your Services

- **Fonte:** 02. Creating a Process of Your Services
- **Duração:** 04:32
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:02]

So, I just want to show you guys how I go about building a process. So, this is going to be the, I'm going to rename this. This is going to be the process for funnel build. And what I do is I basically write out all the steps that are required in order to build and deliver a funnel for the audience that we're working with. Now, it doesn't mean that all of these steps are part of the service. Like for example, we would expect our clients to have this already and we don't really help them with this so much. This is stuff that we teach in the academy, right? It's not really stuff that we do inside the agency. But the agency work would pick up from basically step three. And what this does by outlining it essentially as folders inside of Google Drive as step by steps, it allows me to see all of the processes that I need to record as part of this service offer, as part of this main process that we're selling as a service. So, choosing the funnel strategy essentially, putting together the messaging, building the backend, building the funnel, copywriting for the funnel, and then driving traffic into that. But this could almost be another process in itself, number eight and nine are two different processes. But essentially what I do is I basically open a Google Doc and I start writing out a step by step of, for example, like choosing a hook, I'll write out a step by step in a Google Doc sheet that outlines the steps and I place it inside of the folder. And then what I'm going to do is record a video showing how I go through the steps and that video then goes inside of the folder. And if you want to be even more organized, you can get a VA to basically cut up your video, add captions to it, transcribe it even more so that you have a really detailed process. Now for those of you who don't want to work out of Google Drive, you can also decide to put this inside of a portal like ClickFunnels memberships or some systems like the Psaato allow you to set up stuff like this or Trello. Regardless of the software tool that you use to showcase or to host or to have your processes in, it's always a good idea to create a master sheet under your process drive where you are

## [03:02 - 04:32]

essentially clicking through or showing where the main processes are so people can easily access them. So what this means by working through it like this, it becomes very systematic and I can go through the process once, record it, put it all in organized folders and now that entire service delivery has been processed and it's ready for a production team to come on board and to start training people on how to implement this. And because it's all organized in such a way, training your contractors or a staff member is going to become 10 times easier because now when you onboard them into your business, all that you're doing now is allowing them to go through the videos, so telling them to take their time, go through the videos and then you're getting them to build the process themselves as you oversee it and they can ask questions and then you can assess them on the process and the assessment, the quiz that you put them through after about two or three weeks of being in the business should just be questions that are taken from these processes because if they've gone through that, they should know those answers. So that becomes training, training your staff becomes so much easier because of this and this is the process that I use to put together processes in the business.
